assumptions: The developer has a supported browser, email address, and a Mac, Windows, or Linux workstation.
No 1Password account, desktop app, CLI integration, or Environment exists yet.
The developer is pursuing the individual consumer signup route rather than joining a family account by invitation.

developer_goal: Create a 1Password account, install and integrate the desktop app and CLI, create a developer Environment, and complete the first secure local workflow named by the Developer quickstart.

first_success.outcome: Either a mounted local .env from a 1Password Environment prints expected secrets after biometric approval, or an SSH/Git request is approved through 1Password with a key held in 1Password.
first_success.observable_signal: The Developer quickstart documents cat of the mounted .env after an authorization prompt, or a tested SSH/Git command approved through 1Password.

route_selection.rule: Select one complete Developer quickstart first-workflow route only after the secrets-mount versus SSH/Git peer choice is fixed, and only after current individual signup fields are observed.

candidates.name: Securely load developer secrets via mounted .env
candidates.reason: Official Step 4 presents this as one of two peer first workflows after shared app, CLI, and Environment setup; Mac/Linux mount steps and the terminal cat signal are documented, but the peer SSH/Git route is not ranked below it.

candidates.name: Simplify SSH and Git workflows
candidates.reason: Official Step 4 presents this as a peer first workflow with different OS, key, and Git-service requirements and a different observable terminal.

candidates.name: Administrator or Partner quickstart
candidates.reason: Those guides target team administration or partner integrations and add account-role or partner-program gates outside the developer local-development persona.

required_path.action: Sign up for an individual 1Password account on 1Password.com.

required_fields.label: Individual signup fields
required_fields.notes: Support docs say to sign up on 1Password.com and confirm by email, but do not enumerate current plan, payment, or form controls.

required_path.action: Confirm the account from the 1Password signup email.
required_path.observable_result: The developer can continue account password and Emergency Kit setup.

required_path.action: Choose a strong account password and prepare the Emergency Kit.

required_fields.label: Emergency Kit contents
required_fields.notes: Support docs instruct saving the Emergency Kit with the password filled in.

required_path.observable_result: The individual 1Password account is ready for desktop app sign-in.

required_path.action: Install the 1Password desktop app, sign in, and enable device-based unlock.
required_path.observable_result: The desktop app unlocks with Touch ID, Windows Hello, or Linux system authentication.

required_path.action: Install 1Password CLI and enable Integrate with 1Password CLI in desktop Settings > Developer.
required_path.observable_result: CLI commands can trigger desktop biometric authentication.

required_path.action: Run op vault list and approve the sign-in prompt.
required_path.observable_result: The CLI lists vaults for the authenticated account.

required_path.action: Create a project Environment under Developer > Environments and add variables.
required_path.observable_result: A named Environment contains project secrets.

required_path.action: Complete the selected first secure workflow after Step 4 of the Developer quickstart.

required_fields.label: First workflow choice and workflow-specific settings
required_fields.notes: Secrets mount and SSH/Git remain peer terminals with different required controls.

required_path.observable_result: The selected workflow's documented terminal succeeds.

prerequisites.description: A workstation that can install the 1Password desktop app and 1Password CLI.
For the mounted .env workflow, a Mac or Linux path where Destinations can mount a local .env file.

external_gates.description: Email confirmation is required before the individual account is fully usable.
Family signup can depend on an organizer invitation instead of the individual 1Password.com route.

unavoidable_waits.description: 1Password must deliver the signup confirmation email.

platform_outcomes.description: The desktop app and CLI integration authenticate CLI commands with device-based unlock.
Environments (beta) store project variables that can be mounted or read by CLI/SDK paths.

excluded.item: Reading the Developer quickstart as a measured action
excluded.reason: Documentation reading is evidence, not a product action.
They target different personas and entitlements.

excluded.item: Service accounts for CI/CD and production
excluded.reason: The Developer quickstart explicitly routes automation away from the local biometric path.

sources.title: Developer quickstart
evidence_supported: Desktop app and biometric unlock setup
CLI install and Integrate with 1Password CLI
Peer first workflows: mounted secrets versus SSH/Git

sources.title: Get started with 1Password
evidence_supported: Account password and Emergency Kit guidance

sources.title: Get to know your Emergency Kit
evidence_supported: Emergency Kit preparation during account setup

uncertainties.question: Which Developer quickstart first workflow is the selected shortest path: mounted secrets or SSH and Git?
uncertainties.impact: The peer workflows change OS constraints, required items, commands, external Git handoffs, and the observable terminal.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A corpus-wide route decision that ranks the two official Step 4 peers under the lexicographic selection policy, or vendor guidance that one is the default first success.

uncertainties.question: What exact fields, plan selectors, trial terms, and payment screens appear on the current individual 1Password.com signup?
uncertainties.impact: Unobserved signup controls can add required fields, payment, or trial gates before the Developer quickstart path begins.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated read-only observation of the public individual signup form and any immediate post-submit screens.

uncertainties.question: Is Environments beta available to every fresh individual account in the audit cohort?
uncertainties.impact: If Environments are unavailable, the shared Step 3 and the mounted-secrets terminal are invalid and an alternate vault/item path is required.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Fresh-account confirmation that Developer > Environments is present without an entitlement gate.
